name: Camino Francés: Carrión de los Condes to Frómista Loop
name_original: Carrión de los Condes a Frómista — circular por Camino de las Merinas
description: This 35.0-mile moderate mountain biking route takes you through the flat Castilian plains from Carrión de los Condes to Frómista.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Carrión de los Condes traverse the expansive Meseta landscape within the Tierra de Campos region of Palencia. The terrain is characterized by vast fields of cereals and horticultural crops, interspersed with leafy poplar groves and gentle undulations. Mountain biking in this area often involves gravel and unpaved tracks, offering a mix of flat sections and mellow climbs. Best mountain bike trails around Carrión De Los Condes The most popular mountain bike trail is Carrión de los Condes and Ucie, a 33.5 miles (53.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route features moderate ascents through the agricultural landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Camino Francés: Carrión de los Condes to Frómista Loop, a moderate 34.9 miles (56.3 km) path. This route follows sections of the historic Camino de Santiago, offering views of open fields and small villages. Local mountain bikers also love the Perales and Villanueva del Rebollar Viewpoint Loop, a 25.2 miles (40.5 km) trail leading through agricultural land and offering elevated views, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Mountain biking around Carrión De Los Condes is defined by open Meseta landscapes, extensive cereal fields, and poplar groves, with routes often utilizing gravel and unpaved tracks. The network offers options primarily for moderate ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 14 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Carrión De Los Condes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical terrain like for mountain biking in Carrión de los Condes?
A: The terrain around Carrión de los Condes is characterized by the expansive Meseta landscape, featuring vast fields of cereals and horticultural crops, interspersed with leafy poplar groves. Routes often utilize gravel and unpaved tracks, offering a mix of flat sections and mellow, manageable climbs. It's generally a gently rolling landscape.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families with children?
A: Yes, while most routes are moderate, there is an easy option available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24549744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tejera loop from Villasarracino&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 17 km (10.6 miles) route with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for beginners or families looking for a less challenging ride.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Carrión de los Condes?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Carrión de los Condes is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cold on the open Meseta.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363833228/camino-frances-carrion-de-los-condes-to-fromista-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Francés: Carrión de los Condes to Frómista Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365693471/perales-and-villanueva-del-rebollar-viewpoint-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perales and Villanueva del Rebollar Viewpoint Loop&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363276423/bahillo-a-villota-del-duque-a-gozon-de-ucieza-circular-desde-carrion-de-los-condes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bahillo a Villota del Duque a Gozón de Ucieza — circular desde Carrión de los Condes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see near the mountain bike trails?
A: Carrión de los Condes is rich in history, being on the Camino de Santiago. You can encounter several historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4541387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mayor de Carrión de los Condes&lt;/a&gt; is a historic stone bridge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4930123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Zoilo&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient pilgrims' refuge, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4632666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María del Camino Church&lt;/a&gt; are also notable historical attractions in the town.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region offers wide Castilian skies and open landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365693471/perales-and-villanueva-del-rebollar-viewpoint-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perales and Villanueva del Rebollar Viewpoint Loop&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to offer elevated views over the agricultural land. The expansive Meseta itself provides panoramic vistas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy about the trails in Carrión de los Condes?
A: Mountain bikers often appreciate the open Meseta landscapes, the extensive cereal fields, and the peaceful poplar groves. The routes, which frequently use gravel and unpaved tracks, are generally considered moderate, offering accessible rides through a historic agricultural area.

Q: How long do the mountain bike trails typically take to complete?
A: The duration varies by route and individual pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363522176/carrion-de-los-condes-and-ucie" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrión de los Condes and Ucie&lt;/a&gt; trail, which is about 53.8 km (33.5 miles), typically takes around 4 hours 20 minutes. Shorter routes, like the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24549744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tejera loop from Villasarracino&lt;/a&gt; (17 km), can be completed in under 1 hour 30 minutes.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking here?
A: You'll experience the expansive Meseta landscape, characterized by vast fields of cereals and horticultural crops. The routes are often lined with leafy poplar groves, and the open spaces provide impressive wide Castilian skies, typical of the Tierra de Campos region.

Q: Are there options for longer mountain bike tours from Carrión de los Condes?
A: Yes, some routes are quite substantial. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363276423/bahillo-a-villota-del-duque-a-gozon-de-ucieza-circular-desde-carrion-de-los-condes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bahillo a Villota del Duque a Gozón de Ucieza — circular desde Carrión de los Condes&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 57.6 km (35.8 miles), offering a longer ride through the region's characteristic landscape.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Carrión de los Condes feature relatively mellow and manageable climbs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363522176/carrion-de-los-condes-and-ucie" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrión de los Condes and Ucie&lt;/a&gt; route has an elevation gain of about 345 meters (1,130 feet) over its 53.8 km distance, indicating gentle undulations rather than steep ascents.
tips: Today the road runs over this stone bridge and can be crossed by bike without any problems. Formerly, it could be crossed on foot and it had gates on both sides to charge pilgrims who wanted to use it to cross the Carrión River.
Iron sculpture that commemorates the figure of the pilgrim who passes the Camino de Santiago.

It was part of a Benedictine monastery and this Church is related to the Romanesque currents in Jaca, it was founded by Doña Mayor, Countess of Castile in 1066. It is the purest and most representative Romanesque building in the province of Palencia.
This dirt road next to the main road allows you to follow the road without having to worry about cars. On a mountain bike or gravel bike it is really nice.

It almost looks like a cathedral! Its size, the portico and the rose window that floods the chapel of Santiago with light in the interior stand out.
It can be visited every day and with the pilgrim's credential it costs one euro per person. Children enter free.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for mountain biking on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly flat, traversing the vast Castilian plains of the Tierra de Campos. You'll encounter a mix of unpaved roads, dirt tracks, and dusty farm paths. Some sections run parallel to or directly on paved roads, particularly the P-980. While largely flat, there are very gentle inclines and descents that require some effort.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 56 kilometers (35 miles) long. For mountain biking, it typically takes around 4 hours and 27 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: The route is generally considered of moderate difficulty, making it accessible to a range of cyclists. It's often described as an 'easy' and 'short' stage for cyclists due to its flat profile and lack of significant climbs. However, having good physical fitness and some cycling experience is beneficial.

faqs.question: What historical and cultural sights can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in history, being part of the Camino de Santiago. You'll pass through medieval towns and can visit several Romanesque churches. Notable highlights include the Puente Mayor de Carrión de los Condes, the Santa María del Camino Church, the Pilgrim Statue in Carrión de los Condes, the Church of Saint Martin of Tours (Frómista), and the Church of Santa María la Blanca in Villalcázar de Sirga.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to ride this route, when temperatures are milder. During summer months, the lack of shade on the open plains can make the ride challenging due to intense heat. If riding in summer, it's crucial to carry sufficient water and avoid the hottest parts of the day.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the unpaved sections of this route. However, please ensure your dog is well-behaved, under control, and on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to access this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to mountain bike this route. It primarily follows public paths and roads, including sections of the Camino de Santiago, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to access the Carrión de los Condes a Frómista — circular por Camino de las Merinas route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Carrión de los Condes, which serves as both the starting and ending point for this circular route. Look for public parking areas within the town, especially near the historical center or main access roads.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route extensively uses sections of the Camino Francés - 04 Burgos a León and also incorporates parts of the Camino de las merinas and Camino de Población de Campos. You'll also find it running alongside or on the P-980 road for some stretches.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route connects several villages such as Carrión de los Condes, Villalcázar de Sirga, and Frómista, which are well-equipped to serve pilgrims and cyclists. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and various accommodation options (including albergues and guesthouses) in these towns.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect vast, open views of the quintessential Castilian plains, often featuring golden cereal fields, especially striking in spring and early summer. The expansive landscape offers a sense of tranquility, punctuated by charming villages with traditional architecture and historical sites.
